articles.title: USD/INR snaps three-day losing streak as oil prices recover
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) fails to extend its three-day winning streak against the US Dollar (USD) and corrects sharply on Friday. The USD/INR pair bounces back to near 94.60 from the two-week low of 94.03 posted on Thursday.

articles.title: Crude Oil Recovers as US May Restart Operations to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: June WTI crude oil (CLM26 ) on Thursday closed down -0.27 (-0.28%), and June RBOB gasoline (RBM26 ) closed down -0.0033 (-0.10%). Crude oil and gasoline prices closed lower on Thursday on optimism that a US-Iran peace deal is imminent. The markets await further updates after the US presented a...

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecasts: Rejected at 0.7800, approaches two-month lows at 0.7765
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) resumes its broader bearish trend against the Swiss Franc (CHF) on Friday, reversing Thursday’s gains and reaching levels a few pips above the two-month lows of 0.7765, as the market shifts its focus to the US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report, due later on Friday.

articles.title: US Nonfarm Payrolls set to rise by 62K in April as markets assess Fed rate outlook
articles.expand: The United States (US) Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will release the Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data for April on Friday at 12:30 GMT.

articles.title: USD: Escalation risk supports recovery – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole notes that the US Dollar (USD) has rebounded as hopes for a swift US‑Iran deal fade, with fresh military incidents in the Strait of Hormuz and a restart of US escorts weighing on risk sentiment.

articles.title: USD/CAD softens as jobs data loom, weaker Oil caps Canadian Dollar support
articles.expand: USD/CAD edges lower on Friday and trades around 1.3650 at the time of writing, snapping a two-day winning streak. However, the downside remains limited as investors prefer to stay cautious ahead of the release of the US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report and Canadian employment data later in the day.

articles.title: CAD: Market too aggressive on BoC hikes – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s Elias Haddad expects the Canadian Dollar (CAD) to be cushioned even if markets scale back aggressive Bank of Canada (BoC) rate hike pricing.

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Struggles to lure buyers amid JPY intervention fears; US NFP eyed
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair struggles to capitalize on the previous day's modest gains and trades with a mild negative bias through the first half of the European session on Friday. Despite renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz, investors remain hopeful for a US-Iran peace deal.

articles.title: USD/INR recovers as oil prices recovery weakens Indian Rupee
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) fails to extend its three-day winning streak against the US Dollar (USD) and corrects sharply on Friday. The USD/INR pair bounces back to near 94.60 from the two-week low of 94.03 posted on Thursday.

articles.title: ECB: Data-dependent hiking path under Middle East uncertainty – Nordea
articles.expand: Nordea’s Chief Analyst Jan von Gerich argues that the European Central Bank remains focused on inflation risks despite softer sentiment data and downside growth risks.

articles.title: EUR/CAD holds gains near 1.6050 as Euro advances on hawkish ECB tone
articles.expand: EUR/CAD extends its gains for the third successive day, trading around 1.6040 during the European hours on Friday. The currency cross remains stronger as the Euro (EUR) remains firm against its major peers despite the decline in Germany’s industrial sector activity in March.

articles.title: DXY: Hawkish Fed repricing supports yields – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank analysts point out that stronger US data and hawkish Federal Reserve commentary have pushed Treasury yields higher and supported the Dollar Index.

articles.title: EUR/JPY holds gains above 184.00 on risk-on sentiment, ECB rate hike prospects
articles.expand: The EUR/JPY cross gains traction to around 184.15 during the early European trading hours on Friday. The Euro (EUR) edges higher against the Japanese Yen (JPY) amid improved risk sentiment and hawkish signals from the European Central Bank (ECB).

articles.title: EUR/USD: Oil shock, real rates and conflict risks – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur argues that an end to the Iran conflict and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could support the Euro against the Dollar via relative real-rate dynamics.

articles.expand: The Market Participants Group (MPG) is a senior-level forum for financial market participants to share their views on relevant themes and narratives in financial markets with members of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee.

articles.expand: Japan’s service sector growth slowed notably in April as the impact of the Middle East conflict weighed on demand and business confidence, while sharply higher costs pushed inflation pressures to near-record levels. articles.title: Japan services PMI hits 11-month low in April. War costs bite, input costs 42-month high
articles.expand: Japan's services PMI fell to 51.0 in April from 53.4, an 11-month low, as Middle East war costs drove input inflation to a 42-month high and business confidence hit its lowest since the pandemic. Summary: The S P Global Japan Services PMI fell to 51.0 in April from 53.4 in March, marking the softest rate of expansion in 11 months and a 13th consecu...

articles.title: Japan wage growth streak hits three months, putting June BOJ move in focus
articles.expand: Japan's real wages rose 1.0% in March, a third straight monthly gain; total cash earnings rose 2.7% against a 3.2% consensus and prior; BOJ rate decision due June 15-16. articles.title: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins agreed with FOMC dissenters over statement
articles.expand: Collins said she agreed with colleagues who dissented at last week’s monetary policy gathering over wording in the Fed’s post-meeting statement that suggested the central bank would eventually resume rate reductions.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Bullish near multi-year top amid Iran peace deal hopes, hawkish RBA
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair attracts some buyers for the third consecutive day on Thursday and remains within striking distance of its highest level since June 2022, around the 0.7275-0.7280 region, which it touched the previous day.

articles.title: Asia open: Equities hit records as geopolitical tensions cool and oil dips

Strong earnings momentum, led by mega-cap tech and the AI infrastructure boom, continues to underpin equities. articles.expand: UK 30-year gilts traded as high as 5.78% on Tuesday, the highest level since 1998, while 10-year yields topped 5.10% with markets pricing in nearly three-quarter-point Bank of England (BoE) rate hikes this year.

articles.expand: The USDJPY tumbled sharply last week after intervention fears out of Japan surfaced as the pair pushed above the 160.00 level. articles.title: Swiss CPI Accelerates to 0.6% YoY as Energy Import Costs Rebound
articles.expand: Swiss inflation picked up modestly in April, with headline CPI rising 0.3% month-on-month, driven largely by higher energy and travel-related costs. According to the Federal Statistical Office, increases in petrol, diesel and heating oil prices were key contributors, alongside higher airfares and international package holidays. articles.title: War fears tarnish Metals – Silver (XAG/USD) breaks $75 & Gold (XAU/USD) tests $4,500
articles.expand: XAU/USD, XAG/USD Outlook: Precious metals decline amid escalating Middle East tensions and surging inflation worries.
